target.title: perf(plugins,gateway): async-ify channels.status + messages.send discovery (and split WhatsApp transport closes)
target.body:
Closes #78100. Companion to (not blocked by) #78101 — that PR coalesces repeated calls within a 2 s TTL as a near-term win; this PR is the deeper structural fix #78100 proposed as item 2.

## Summary

Async-ify the persisted-read hot path inside `loadPluginRegistrySnapshotWithMetadata` and the auto-enable resolver inside `messages.send` so `channels.status`, `channels.start`, and inbound dispatch no longer block the Node event loop on `realpathSync` / `statSync` / `readFileSync` while verifying the persisted plugin index. Cold-path discovery yields once via `setImmediate` so other I/O can interleave with the still-sync walk.

Adds:
- `loadPluginRegistrySnapshotWithMetadataAsync`
- `hasMissingPersistedPluginSourceAsync`
- `hasStalePersistedPluginMetadataAsync`

with inflight-promise dedup so concurrent callers don't redo the syscalls.

Also includes (smaller, related):
- WhatsApp auto-reply monitor: split transport-class WS handshake closes (408, 428, 5xx) from lifecycle-class closes (401 logged-out, 440 conflict). Transport closes now retry inside the running monitor's existing reconnect loop instead of throwing out and triggering a full plugin restart that re-runs discovery (and re-pays the sync cost above). Only logged-out / conflict still bubble up to relinking. Adds `event` / `closeClass` / `phase` structured log fields so transport recoveries are greppable separately from lifecycle restarts.

## Why this approach

The repo's `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md` says *"Do not add persistent metadata caches for discovery, manifest registries, installed-index reconstruction"* — this PR doesn't. It keeps the freshness-first design intact and just removes the synchronous-blocking property of the read path, which is what burns event-loop time on slow filesystems. #78101's 2 s TTL is a deliberately short coalescing window; this PR addresses the underlying cost so even a single cold call is loop-friendly.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On Kubernetes / OKE with a PVC-backed `~/.openclaw`, an inbound WhatsApp message burst causes event-loop blocks of 1.0–20.8 s, dispatch latencies of 17–27 s per turn, and (eventually) 408 keepalive misses that cascade into channel restarts.
- Environment tested: OKE cluster on a single `VM.Standard.A1.Flex` node (2 OCPU / 11.4 GiB / arm64). Pod limits `2 / 4Gi`, requests `500m / 1Gi`. PVC: `oci-bv` block-volume storage class, 5 GiB, mounted at `~/.openclaw`. Image base `node:24-bookworm-slim`. 8 plugins loaded. Agent model `openai/gpt-5.5`. WhatsApp Web personal pairing, `dmPolicy: "allowlist"`.
- Steps run after the patch:
  1. Build A (ablation): `vendor/openclaw` reset to commit `c874c0863a` (the base of this branch on `main` before the 4 commits). Verified the post-build `dist/` does NOT contain `loadPluginRegistrySnapshotWithMetadataAsync` or `isTransportClassConnectError` symbols.
  2. Build B (this PR): `vendor/openclaw` at HEAD of this branch. Same Dockerfile, same `pnpm install` and `pnpm build` steps. Verified `dist/` DOES contain those symbols.
  3. Helm upgrade rolled the same release between Build A and Build B with PVC preserved (no re-pairing needed). Identical resources and config.
  4. Workload: 9 self-DM WhatsApp text messages within 10 s. All passed the dmPolicy allowlist gate.
  5. Captured: gateway diagnostic stream (`liveness warning` events with `eventLoopDelayMaxMs`, `eventLoopUtilization`, `cpuCoreRatio`, queue depth, `phase`) and structured log entries for each `inbound message` and `auto-reply sent (text)` (with `durationMs`).
- Evidence: redacted runtime logs from the gateway diagnostic stream and structured channel logs (Build A vs Build B, same Helm release).
  - Build A liveness warning, sustained burst (queue depth 7): `eventLoopDelayMaxMs=2655 ms`, `phase=channels.whatsapp.start-account`, `recentPhases=...sidecars.model-prewarm:1021ms`.
  - Build A liveness warning, worst-case 9-msg burst (queue depth 8): `eventLoopDelayMaxMs=20837.3 ms`, `eventLoopUtilization=0.751`, `cpuCoreRatio=0.822`.
  - Build A liveness warning, idle/cold start: `eventLoopDelayMaxMs=1096 ms`.
  - Build B equivalent burst: no liveness warnings exceeding `eventLoopDelayMaxMs=305 ms`. `cpuCoreRatio` returns to ~0.5 between bursts.
  - Build A reply latencies (3 of 9 burst): `21:06:38 inbound → 21:07:05 reply = 27 s`, `21:06:44 inbound → 21:07:05 reply = 21 s`, `21:06:48 inbound → 21:07:05 reply = 17 s`.
  - Build B reply latencies (warm): `durationMs:5–9` for delivery, full turn ~5–9 s bound by model latency, not gateway.
- Observed result:
  - eventLoopDelayMaxMs (worst-case burst): **20,837 ms → not observed** (~70× lower).
  - eventLoopDelayMaxMs (sustained burst): **2655 ms → 305 ms** (~9× lower).
  - User-visible reply latency (burst): **17–27 s → 5–9 s** (~3–5× lower).
  - Transport-fix half: structurally verifiable in code (post-fix, transport-class close codes stay in the reconnect loop with `runId` preserved); a concrete 408 keepalive miss didn't fall inside the test window. The two readings together are consistent with the keepalive-miss correlation table in #78100 (`event-loop block 56–107 s → 408 disconnect`).
- Not tested:
  - Native Linux/macOS / NVMe SSD impact: should be a no-op cost (the underlying syscalls return in <1 ms there, so the async wrapper just adds a microtask). The fix matters where syscalls are 10–100× slower (PVC-backed K8s, WSL2 9P, NFS, container overlays).
  - Telegram / Discord / other channels: the perf path covers all `channels.status` and `messages.send` callers, so other channels should also benefit, but the burst test only exercised WhatsApp.
  - Long-window 408 cascade: requires sustained event-loop pressure that takes ≥3 minutes on Build A; not captured here.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts` — 6/6 pass after rebase.
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor-state.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/web-auto-reply-monitor.test.ts` — 27/27 pass after rebase.
- [x] `pnpm build` — green on rebased HEAD.
- [x] Live K8s/OKE proof (above): A/B image swap, same Helm release, PVC preserved, 9-message WhatsApp burst.
- [ ] Native Linux/macOS regression check (not run by the author; CI lanes will cover this).
target.author: fede-kamel
